scenarios.name: Failed sign-ins / brute force
scenarios.purpose: Failed logon attempts that reveal brute-force, password-spray, or bad-credential activity (4625).

scenarios.name: Account lockouts
scenarios.purpose: Accounts locked out after repeated failed logons (4740).

scenarios.name: Successful sign-ins
scenarios.purpose: Successful account logons for session and access auditing (4624).

scenarios.name: Explicit credentials / RunAs
scenarios.purpose: Logons using explicit credentials such as RunAs (4648); useful for lateral-movement hunting.

scenarios.name: Special-privilege logon
scenarios.purpose: Logons granted sensitive privileges, including administrative equivalents (4672).

scenarios.name: Account management
scenarios.purpose: User account create, enable, disable, delete, password-reset, and change events (4720-4781).

scenarios.name: Group membership changes
scenarios.purpose: Security-group member add and remove events (4728, 4729, 4732, 4733, 4756).

scenarios.name: Kerberos & ticket activity
scenarios.purpose: Kerberos TGT and service-ticket requests and failures (4768, 4769, 4771, 4772).

scenarios.name: Credential validation (NTLM)
scenarios.purpose: NTLM credential validation events on the authenticating server (4776).

scenarios.name: Audit / security policy changes
scenarios.purpose: Audit-log clear (1102) and audit-policy change (4719) events; classic defense-evasion signals.

scenarios.name: Logon activity triage
scenarios.purpose: Color-coded logon outcomes in one view: successful logon (4624), failed logon (4625), account lockout (4740), and explicit-credential / RunAs logon (4648). Requires the Logon and Account-Management audit subcategories.

scenarios.name: Account compromise timeline
scenarios.purpose: Reconstructs a takeover end-to-end, color-coded: failed logons / spray (4625), the successful breakthrough (4624), privilege escalation (4672), persistence via account and group changes (4720, 4722, 4724, 4728, 4732, 4756), and audit-log clear to cover tracks (1102).

scenarios.name: Account lockout investigation
scenarios.purpose: The full lockout story in one view, color-coded: the failed logons that caused it (4625), the lockout (4740), the unlock (4767), and an admin password reset (4724).

scenarios.name: Failed logon - network & RDP triage
scenarios.purpose: Color-coded 4625 failed logons by access channel: network credential spray (LogonType 3, Red), interactive RDP brute force (LogonType 10, Orange), and workstation-unlock retries (LogonType 7, Blue). Splits a high-volume 4625 stream into distinct attack vectors at a glance. Requires Audit Logon. ATT&CK T1110, T1110.003.

scenarios.name: Kerberoasting - RC4 service-ticket request
scenarios.purpose: 4769 Kerberos TGS requests using RC4-HMAC-NT encryption (TicketEncryptionType = 23, hex 0x17). In a modern AES-only Active Directory, RC4 TGS requests targeting user-account SPNs are the primary Kerberoasting offline-cracking indicator. Requires Audit Kerberos Service Ticket Operations on domain controllers. ATT&CK T1558.003.

scenarios.name: Kerberos service-ticket encryption triage
scenarios.purpose: Color-coded 4769 TGS stream focused on anomalous encryption: RC4-HMAC-NT (decimal 23, hex 0x17; DarkRed, Kerberoasting risk) and DES variants (decimal 1 or 3; Red, obsolete or downgrade). Expected AES traffic is not highlighted so anomalies stand out. Requires Audit Kerberos Service Ticket Operations on domain controllers. ATT&CK T1558.003, T1600.001.

scenarios.name: Pass-the-Hash - NTLM network logon
scenarios.purpose: 4624 successful network logons (LogonType 3) authenticated via NTLM rather than Kerberos. In a modern Active Directory environment NTLM network logons are anomalous and the primary Pass-the-Hash indicator; both predicates must match (AND). Requires Audit Logon. ATT&CK T1550.002.

scenarios.name: Explicit credentials - remote target (lateral movement)
scenarios.purpose: 4648 explicit-credential logons where TargetServerName is not 'localhost', identifying RunAs or credential-stuffing used to reach a remote machine. Eliminates high-volume background noise from local service starts and scheduled tasks while surfacing the lateral-movement signal. Requires Audit Logon. ATT&CK T1550, T1078.
